prompt:
The nightly loader talks imp/1 to the import service: HELLO, SCHEMA, a run of ROW lines, COMMIT. Last week ops turned on connection reuse so the loader runs all of a store's feed files over one connection instead of redialing per file, and since then the second import on a reused connection always fails with 'unexpected message: HELLO'; fresh connections work. Every first import is clean — the loader then greets again for the next file and gets slapped. Ops rolled reuse back for now, but redialing per file is exactly the connection churn we were trying to kill.

Repro is easy in-process: drive two back-to-back imports through one session and the second greeting errors out. The wire framing didn't change with the rollout, and the same build works fine when every file gets its own connection, so it smells like session state, but I've stared at the phase checks and each one reads right to me.

Find it and fix it; `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` has to come out green, and session_test.go is off-limits. (Ledger note: nearest seed is go-telewire, which is wire ENCODING perf — this one is pure session-sequencing semantics, no encoding layer, and no other FSM seed covers protocol sessions.)
